title: SPH Parameter Tuning: Density, Pressure, Viscosity, Stability
summary: Physics-domain primer for tuning Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics (SPH) simulations generated by Mosaic's physics/fluid-create, simulation/fluid, and simulation/smoke tools. SPH is a Lagrangian particle method that approximates Navier-Stokes via kernel-weighted neighborhoods; parameter choice governs whether a scene simulates stably or explodes. Covers stable value ranges for restDensity, gasConstant, viscosity, smoothingRadius, mass, and damping; equation-of-state selection (linear vs Tait); CFL timestep rule; negative-pressure clamping; Unity-specific gravity-vs-scale traps; boundary handling; neighborhood search; and the five observed failure modes from the Gemini fluid session (outward explosion, wall tunneling, clumping, NaN propagation, hovering fluid). Orthogonal to GPU plumbing — see kb-rendering-compute-buffer-lifecycle for buffer/dispatch mechanics. Use this entry whenever an LLM must pick, defend, or debug SPH numeric parameters on a fresh scene.

llmGuidance.when_generating_a_fresh_sph_water_scene: Default to restDensity=1000, gasConstant=3.0, viscosity=3.5, smoothingRadius=2x particle spacing, mass = restDensity * particle_volume, damping=0.95, velocityCap=10 m/s, substeps=4. Clamp pressure>=0 and density>=0.5*restDensity in the compute kernel before touching forces.
llmGuidance.when_generating_a_smoke_or_gas_scene: Use restDensity=1.2 (air), gasConstant=1.5-2.0 (softer than water), viscosity=1.0, and disable surface tension. Negative buoyancy for smoke is achieved by biasing gravity to positive Y scaled by (1 - density/ambient), not by flipping Physics.gravity.
llmGuidance.when_fluid_explodes_outward_on_spawn: Almost always unclamped negative pressure. In the compute kernel enforce pressure = max(0, k*(density - restDensity)) or switch to the Tait equation of state: pressure = k*((density/restDensity)^7 - 1) with the same max(0,...) clamp.
llmGuidance.when_particles_tunnel_through_walls: CFL is violated. Either reduce simulated dt (increase substep count per FixedUpdate: 4 -> 8 -> 16) or cap velocity magnitude before integration. Rule: dt <= 0.4 * smoothingRadius / v_max. Verify substepping actually runs by logging dt_sim vs Time.fixedDeltaTime.
llmGuidance.when_particles_clump_into_a_single_point: Tensile instability. Root causes, in order: (1) negative pressure allowed through; (2) restDensity set too low, causing equilibrium pressure to be negative on average; (3) smoothing radius smaller than inter-particle spacing, so each particle sees zero neighbors and density reads ~0. Clamp density to 0.5*restDensity minimum and recheck h.
llmGuidance.when_the_user_specifies_scale_other_than: Unity's Physics.gravity = (0, -9.81, 0) is expressed in m/s^2 at Unity world scale. If the simulation spaces particles 0.1 m apart in world units but the author intended 'centimeter-scale water', either: (a) adjust effective_g = Physics.gravity.y * (sim_scale / world_scale), or (b) leave gravity alone and re-derive particle mass from the intended real volume. Do one, not both.
llmGuidance.when_nan_appears_in_the_particle_buffer: A single divide-by-zero (density -> 0) produces NaN pressure, which propagates to neighbors within one step and contaminates the whole buffer within ~3 frames. Insert an explicit isnan / isinf check per particle per frame and reset offenders to rest state. Also clamp density >= 0.5*restDensity at the source.
llmGuidance.when_the_fluid_visibly_oscillates_or_cha: Stiff equation of state at too-large dt. Options: (1) raise damping 0.95 -> 0.98, (2) switch from linear pressure P=k*(rho-rho0) to Tait P=k*((rho/rho0)^7-1) which is stiffer near rest but softer in the tensile regime, (3) lower gasConstant and accept some compressibility, (4) shrink dt.
llmGuidance.when_the_fluid_hovers_and_does_not_fall: Gravity scaled by mass but applied before density weighting, or scale mismatch between Physics.gravity and sim units. In the compute kernel gravity force should be F_grav = density * g (mass density x acceleration), not mass x g, when the accumulator divides by density later. Verify by disabling all forces except gravity and checking particles free-fall at ~9.81 m/s^2 at scale=1.
llmGuidance.when_asked_for_boundary_behavior: Simplest stable boundary is reflect-on-wall plus velocity damping on the reflected component (v_n_out = -restitution * v_n_in, typical restitution=0.3-0.5). Ghost particles and SDF-based repulsion are more accurate but out of scope for generated MVP scripts. Whichever path you take, process the boundary AFTER position integration so particles don't sit inside the wall accumulating force.

whatSphIs.definition: Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics is a Lagrangian (particle-following) method for approximating the Navier-Stokes equations. Each particle carries position, velocity, mass, and sampled fluid quantities (density, pressure). Field quantities at a particle are reconstructed by summing kernel-weighted contributions from neighbors within a smoothing radius h.
whatSphIs.contrastWithEulerian: Eulerian (grid) methods like Jos Stam's Stable Fluids store velocity/density on fixed cells and advect values through them; they handle smoke and unbounded domains well but require a grid that covers the whole domain. SPH places compute where the fluid is, scales naturally to free-surface liquids, and couples easily with rigid bodies — at the cost of O(N) neighborhood queries and a harder time with incompressibility.
whatSphIs.solverFamily: The generated Mosaic scripts use the Muller 2003 weakly-compressible formulation: density accumulated via Poly6 kernel, pressure force via Spiky kernel gradient, viscosity force via a Laplacian kernel. This is the simplest stable SPH that runs at interactive rates on a GPU.

coreQuantities.perParticle: position (float3), velocity (float3), density (float, computed), pressure (float, derived from density), mass (float, constant per material), acceleration (float3, accumulator zeroed each step). Typical C# struct size including float4 padding: 48-64 bytes.
coreQuantities.derivation: density = sum_j mass_j * W_poly6(r_ij, h). pressure = equation-of-state(density, restDensity, gasConstant). acceleration = (pressureForce + viscosityForce + externalForce) / density. position += velocity * dt (then velocity += acceleration * dt, or swap order for semi-implicit Euler).

kernelFunctions.poly6: W_poly6(r, h) = (315 / (64*pi*h^9)) * (h^2 - r^2)^3 for 0 <= r <= h, else 0. Smooth and symmetric; used for density accumulation where gradient behavior does not matter.
kernelFunctions.spiky: grad W_spiky(r, h) = -(45 / (pi*h^6)) * (h - r)^2 * r_hat for 0 <= r <= h. Non-zero gradient at r=0, which is why it is used for pressure-gradient force — Poly6's gradient vanishes at the center and would cause particles to pass through each other.
kernelFunctions.viscosityLaplacian: lap W_visc(r, h) = (45 / (pi*h^6)) * (h - r) for 0 <= r <= h. Positive everywhere inside the kernel, which damps relative motion smoothly.
kernelFunctions.whyThreeKernels: Each term in Navier-Stokes has different derivative-order requirements and numerical quirks. Mixing Poly6 for density, Spiky for pressure gradient, and the viscosity Laplacian above is the canonical Muller 2003 choice; substituting one for another is a well-known source of instability.
kernelFunctions.smoothingRadius: h defines the neighborhood. Rule of thumb: h = 2 * average_particle_spacing so each particle has roughly 30-50 neighbors in 3D. Too small: sparse neighborhoods, density reads near zero, tensile collapse. Too large: over-smoothing, loss of features, and O(N^2) creep as neighbor counts explode.

water_1m_scale.gasConstant: 3.0 to 5.0 (higher = stiffer + more stable but chattier + needs smaller dt)
water_1m_scale.surfaceTension: 0.0728 N/m at room temperature. At sub-meter scales this is significant; at Unity-world meter scale often safely omitted.
water_1m_scale.massPerParticle: density * particleVolume. For a uniform grid of spacing dx in 3D: mass = restDensity * dx^3.
water_1m_scale.damping: 0.9 to 0.98 (multiplier on velocity each step to absorb numerical noise)
water_1m_scale.velocityCap: 10 m/s for water at meter scale; clamp |v| to keep CFL bounded

smoke_gas.buoyancy: F = (ambientDensity - particleDensity) * g, opposite sign to gravity

parameterRanges.derivedConsistency: mass * particleCount should approximately equal restDensity * emitterVolume. If you choose restDensity=1000 and a 1 m^3 box with 1000 particles, mass must be 1.0 kg. Authors regularly set mass=1.0 and particleCount=65536 in a 1 m^3 box, giving an effective density of 65536 kg/m^3 and guaranteeing a stiff explosion.

pressureComputation.linearEOS: pressure = gasConstant * (density - restDensity). Simple and fast. Unstable when density drops below restDensity: pressure goes negative, force reverses, and particles accelerate toward each other until the buffer NaN's. Only safe with an explicit pressure = max(0, pressure) clamp.
pressureComputation.taitEOS: pressure = (gasConstant * restDensity / gamma) * ((density/restDensity)^gamma - 1), gamma=7 for water. Much stiffer near rest density, softer in the tensile regime, and naturally near-zero below rest. Still clamp pressure >= 0 as a safety net.
pressureComputation.criticalRule: ALWAYS clamp pressure >= 0 in the compute kernel before computing the pressure-gradient force. This single line prevents the most common SPH failure mode: 'negative-pressure implosion' where low-density surface particles pull inward and the fluid collapses into a point.
pressureComputation.symmetrization: The force on particle i from neighbor j uses a symmetric form: F_p = -mass_j * (P_i + P_j) / (2 * density_j) * grad W. The average of pressures avoids the asymmetric-force-pair bug that otherwise violates momentum conservation.

timestepStability.cflCondition: Courant-Friedrichs-Lewy: dt <= C * h / v_max, with C around 0.4 for weakly-compressible SPH. If v_max is 10 m/s and h is 0.2 m, dt must stay under ~8 ms. Unity's default FixedUpdate at 50 Hz is 20 ms — already too coarse for many water sims without substepping.
timestepStability.forceCondition: Separate constraint: dt <= 0.25 * sqrt(h / |a_max|) guards against overshoot from large pressure spikes. In practice the velocity CFL dominates unless a collider moves into the fluid at high speed.
timestepStability.substepping: Canonical pattern: FixedUpdate runs an outer loop, which iterates 4-8 inner SPH steps at dt_sim = fixedDeltaTime / substeps. Log dt_sim and v_max each frame; if dt_sim * v_max > 0.4 * h, warn and auto-increase substep count. The Gemini session spent several iterations tracking down tunneling that was actually a substep count of 1.

scaleMismatchUnity.problem: Physics.gravity.y = -9.81 is expressed in meters per second squared at Unity world scale. SPH authors frequently intend a different physical scale (a 'tabletop' scene with 10 cm water, or an 'ocean' scene with 100 m waves) while leaving the Transform scale at 1. If particle mass and smoothing radius are chosen for the intended physical scale but gravity is left at world-scale -9.81, the simulation runs at the wrong timescale.
scaleMismatchUnity.twoValidFixes: (a) Scale gravity: effective_g = Physics.gravity.y * (sim_scale / world_scale). If sim_scale is 0.1 m (tabletop) and world_scale is 1 m, apply g = -0.981. (b) Keep gravity at -9.81 and re-derive mass, restDensity, and h for Unity-world units; this is the cleanest approach because it keeps Physics.gravity consistent with Rigidbody buoyancy couplings.
scaleMismatchUnity.antiPattern: Applying BOTH fixes is the bug seen in the Gemini session: author scaled gravity AND used world-scale Physics.gravity in the buoyancy coupling, giving a factor-of-sim_scale discrepancy between fluid and floaters. Pick one scaling convention and enforce it at the script boundary.

boundaryHandling.reflectOnWall: After position integration, if p.y < box.min.y: p.y = box.min.y; v.y = -restitution * v.y; with restitution=0.3-0.5. Same for all six faces of an AABB. Simple, stable, and sufficient for demo-grade containers.
boundaryHandling.distanceRepulsion: An alternative: add a repulsive force proportional to 1/distance^2 near walls. Smoother but tunable and can destabilize if the coefficient fights gravity.
boundaryHandling.ghostParticles: Accurate: mirror interior particles across the wall as virtual neighbors so the density field does not see a vacuum at the boundary. Expensive and complex; skip for MVP.
boundaryHandling.sdfColliders: For arbitrary collider shapes, sample an SDF at each particle; push particles out along the gradient when SDF < 0. Preferred for production but requires SDF generation infrastructure.
boundaryHandling.orderingRule: Always apply boundary correction AFTER position integration and BEFORE the next density accumulation. Applying before integration lets particles drift into walls and re-enter from inside, producing a feedback loop that fails silently.

neighborhoodSearch.uniformGrid: Cell size = h. Each particle hashes to one cell. Neighbor search iterates the particle's cell + 26 neighbors (3x3x3 in 3D). Typical per-particle neighbor count: 30-50. Uniform grid is GPU-friendly because cell buckets can be built by sort + scan on the buffer.
neighborhoodSearch.neighborCap: Generated scripts cap neighbors at 32-64 per particle to bound per-thread register pressure. Over the cap, discard further neighbors — slight accuracy loss, large stability gain.
neighborhoodSearch.spatialHash: For very non-uniform distributions, replace grid with a spatial hash. The GPU implementation is nearly identical; only the cell-index function changes. Rarely needed for bounded liquid sims.

failureModes.symptom: Fluid explodes outward on spawn (particles fly to infinity within one frame).
failureModes.cause: Unclamped negative pressure combined with gasConstant too high, OR initial density reads far above restDensity because particles were packed closer than h assumes.
failureModes.fix: Clamp pressure >= 0 in the kernel. Reduce gasConstant to 3.0 while tuning. Verify initial particle spacing matches h/2.

failureModes.cause: CFL violated: dt * v_max > 0.4 * h. Boundary response fires after the particle has already crossed.
failureModes.fix: Increase substep count (4 -> 8 -> 16). Cap velocity magnitude (|v| <= 10 m/s). Reduce simulated dt.

failureModes.symptom: Particles visibly clump into a single point or a few dense streaks.
failureModes.cause: Tensile instability. Negative pressure was allowed through, or smoothing radius smaller than particle spacing caused density to read zero.
failureModes.fix: Clamp pressure >= 0. Clamp density >= 0.5 * restDensity. Verify h = 2 * particle_spacing.

failureModes.symptom: NaN spreads through the buffer after a few seconds.
failureModes.cause: Divide-by-zero when density -> 0 produces NaN pressure; the symmetric pressure-force term propagates NaN to every neighbor within one step.
failureModes.fix: Clamp density to a floor (0.5 * restDensity) at the source. Add isnan/isinf guard per particle per frame and reset offenders to rest velocity at rest position.

failureModes.symptom: Fluid hovers and refuses to fall, or falls at wrong rate.
failureModes.cause: Gravity scaled inconsistently with particle mass or world scale. Often: 'gravity in code = Physics.gravity.y' AND 'mass = 1 kg but intended density implies mass 0.001'.
failureModes.fix: Pick one of: scale gravity (effective_g = Physics.gravity.y * sim_scale / world_scale) OR re-derive mass from intended density and particle volume. Enforce which at script header.

failureModes.symptom: Visible checkerboard or regular structured artifact on the surface.
failureModes.cause: Smoothing radius too small relative to particle spacing; kernel sums fail to overlap between adjacent particles.
failureModes.fix: Increase h. Check that each particle sees at least 20 neighbors (log average neighbor count from the compute shader during debug).

failureModes.symptom: Simulation oscillates visibly — particles chatter at rest.
failureModes.cause: Equation of state too stiff for the chosen dt. Linear EOS with gasConstant=5 at dt=0.02 will ring.
failureModes.fix: Raise damping to 0.98. Switch to Tait EOS (gamma=7). Lower gasConstant. Shrink dt.

stabilityGuards.densityFloor: density = max(density, 0.5 * restDensity). Prevents the divide-by-zero that seeds NaN propagation.
stabilityGuards.pressureFloor: pressure = max(pressure, 0). The single most important clamp in SPH; prevents tensile collapse.
stabilityGuards.velocityCap: if (length(v) > v_cap) v = normalize(v) * v_cap. Bounds CFL regardless of what the pressure solver does.
stabilityGuards.nanReset: Each frame, check isnan(p.x) || isnan(v.x) || isnan(density). If true, reset: p = restPosition, v = 0, density = restDensity. Costs 3 bool checks per particle; saves the buffer from unbounded NaN propagation.
stabilityGuards.damping: v *= damping (0.95 typical). Absorbs high-frequency numerical noise that otherwise drives CFL violations.
stabilityGuards.substepAutoTune: Log dt * v_max / h each frame. If > 0.4, increase substep count; if < 0.1, decrease (within a min/max). Self-tuning removes the 'bad on one machine, fine on another' class of bug.

substeppingPattern.innerLoop: 4-8 substeps per FixedUpdate, dt_sim = dt_fixed / substeps. Compute shader dispatched once per substep for each kernel (density, forces, integrate, boundary).
substeppingPattern.loggingContract: Expose dt_sim, substepCount, v_max, and CFL ratio (dt_sim * v_max / h) as inspector fields or a diagnostic struct. Gemini session lost ~40 minutes failing to observe that substepCount was silently 1.

decisionRules.scenario: Fluid explodes outward on spawn or during collision.
decisionRules.rule: Clamp pressure >= 0 in the compute kernel BEFORE computing pressure force. Reduce gasConstant to 3.0. Verify initial particle spacing matches h assumption.
decisionRules.evidence: Gemini fluid session 2026-04-20: generated kernel used pressure = gasConstant * (density - restDensity) with no clamp; spawning produced negative pressure at free-surface particles and the column inverted within two frames.

decisionRules.rule: Reduce dt_sim via substepping (4 -> 8 -> 16) OR cap velocity magnitude to bound CFL. Rule: dt <= 0.4 * h / v_max.
decisionRules.evidence: Gemini fluid session: tunneling at restitution=0.5 with substepCount=1 and v_max=15 m/s; fixed by substepCount=8 and velocityCap=8 m/s.

decisionRules.scenario: User specifies particle scale != 1 m (tabletop or ocean sim).
decisionRules.rule: Pick one: (a) scale gravity effective_g = Physics.gravity.y * (sim_scale / world_scale), OR (b) re-derive mass from intended real density and particle volume while keeping Unity-world gravity. Never both.
decisionRules.evidence: Gemini fluid session: both fixes applied simultaneously, producing a factor-of-10 mismatch between fluid fall rate and buoyancy response on a rigidbody floater.

decisionRules.rule: First raise damping 0.95 -> 0.98. If still oscillating, switch linear EOS to Tait (gamma=7). If still oscillating, shrink dt. Only lower gasConstant if the fluid should be visibly compressible.
decisionRules.evidence: Standard weakly-compressible SPH tuning order from Monaghan 1992 and Koschier et al 2019.

decisionRules.rule: Clamp density >= 0.5 * restDensity at source. Add per-particle isnan check and reset offenders to rest state. Do NOT hope it self-corrects — symmetric pressure force propagates NaN to neighbors within one step.
decisionRules.evidence: Muller 2003 density formula has no floor; Koschier 2019 tutorial recommends the floor + reset pattern for production stability.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Column of water collapses into a single bright dot at the bottom.
commonPitfalls.cause: Tensile instability from unclamped negative pressure.
commonPitfalls.fix: pressure = max(0, k*(density - restDensity)) in the compute kernel. Consider switching to Tait EOS.

commonPitfalls.cause: Gravity accumulated as mass * g but divided out by density later, producing g / density instead of g. Or: sim_scale / world_scale mismatch.
commonPitfalls.fix: Apply gravity as F_grav = density * g (so acceleration = g after dividing by density). Audit scale once at script boundary.

commonPitfalls.cause: Not SPH — instancing keyword missing; see kb-rendering-compute-buffer-lifecycle. SPH simulation is running correctly, rendering is broken.
commonPitfalls.fix: Enable PROCEDURAL_INSTANCING_ON on the material. See related KB.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Particle count keeps increasing each play-mode enter.
commonPitfalls.cause: Not SPH tuning — ComputeBuffer leak. See kb-rendering-compute-buffer-lifecycle.
commonPitfalls.fix: OnEnable/OnDisable allocation pattern. See related KB.

commonPitfalls.symptom: Fluid simulates fine at 60 FPS but explodes at 30 FPS.
commonPitfalls.cause: Framerate coupled to dt; CFL violated when dt doubles.
commonPitfalls.fix: Use FixedUpdate with substepping, not Update-based time. Log dt_sim and auto-raise substep count if CFL exceeds 0.4.

versionDeltas.change: Unity Jobs + Burst compiler reached stability. CPU SPH of ~10K particles becomes viable without a compute shader, which matters for platforms where compute is unreliable (some mobile, WebGL 2).
versionDeltas.migrationNote: Optional. GPU compute shader path remains preferred for >10K particles. Consider Jobs-based CPU fallback only when targeting compute-unfriendly platforms.

externalReferences.title: Unity Manual — FixedUpdate and physics timestep

sources.title: Muller, Charypar, Gross — Particle-Based Fluid Simulation for Interactive Applications (SIGGRAPH/Eurographics SCA 2003)
Monaghan — Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics (Annual Review of Astronomy and Astrophysics, 1992)
Koschier, Bender, Solenthaler, Teschner — Smoothed Particle Hydrodynamics Techniques for the Physics Based Simulation of Fluids and Solids (Eurographics 2019 Tutorial)

authorshipNotes: Content authored by Mosaic team from three open-access academic sources: Muller Charypar Gross 2003 (author's own page PDF, routinely cited and redistributed for educational use), Monaghan 1992 (Annual Reviews, widely available open PDF), and Koschier Bender Solenthaler Teschner 2019 Eurographics tutorial (author-hosted PDF, open educational material). Physical constants (water restDensity 1000 kg/m^3, surface tension 0.0728 N/m, gravitational acceleration 9.81 m/s^2) and algorithm steps (Poly6, Spiky, Tait EOS gamma=7 for water, CFL constant ~0.4) are scientific facts and are not copyrightable. Explanatory prose, decision rules, llmGuidance scenarios, commonPitfalls, failureModes taxonomy, and the Unity-specific scale-mismatch analysis are original Mosaic editorial content derived from the Gemini fluid-sim session evidence (_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/gap-analysis/fluid.md section 4D KB-fluid-02) and independent synthesis of the cited academic sources. No Unity documentation is reproduced here; the simulation physics is Unity-version-agnostic and this entry deliberately avoids paraphrasing Unity Manual pages. Source PDFs are cited for runtime lookup only and are NOT reproduced inline.
